heading: The path so far
body: Milestones in narrative order — not strict chronology. Each entry surfaces the tools and tech learned at that point.

items.title: First touched design
items.body: Photoshop, After Effects, the early Adobe-suite curiosity that turned into a serious hobby.

items.title: Graduated high school
items.body: Started college at IGNOU + a parallel course in 3ds Max, Photoshop, After Effects, and Maya. The 3D pipeline foundation.

items.title: First serious dive into JavaScript
items.body: Built libkcdxenhtml — a library for building XenHTML home-screen replacements for jailbroken iPhones. Started learning React. Began freelancing.

items.title: COVID — co-built Nitroless
items.body: Cydia-style repo GUI system for distributing custom stickers + emojis across messaging apps. Shipped a Swift app + keyboard, Jetpack Compose app + keyboard, a macOS app, and an Electron app — community-driven, multi-platform.

items.title: First job — Vidhema Technologies
items.body: 3 months. Worked across Node.js, React, and Angular — first time shipping production code on someone else's stack.

items.title: Moved to Canada — built United Walls solo
items.body: Wallpaper-sharing platform with a SwiftUI iOS app, a Jetpack Compose Android app, a Grammy bot for Telegram, and a self-hosted Node/Express/Mongoose backend on a Raspberry Pi. First deep dive into Docker and self-hosting.

items.title: Joined Unimedia Technology
items.body: Started with Angular (had been React-only before), then picked up .NET for APIs, PHP, Ionic, and a wider full-stack practice.

items.title: Shipped SpotlightSearch
items.body: Android app inspired by macOS Spotlight — quick app launcher, contact search with call/SMS/WhatsApp shortcuts, inline calculator + unit conversions, Google search suggestions. Pure Kotlin. Shipped to the Play Store.

items.title: Started InfluentialLauncher
items.body: Custom Android launcher experiment in pure Kotlin — Gradle Kotlin DSL build, full-stack ground-up rewrite of the launcher surface.

items.title: AI-assisted coding
items.body: Started experimenting. Learned the limitations and the sharp edges, but also where it genuinely accelerates work.

items.title: Started KCDSearch — cross-platform search
items.body: Personal-knowledge search across the apps I use. Web client first (TypeScript, Jan 2026), then native Android (Kotlin, Feb 2026), then native Apple (Swift, Mar 2026). Three platforms, one product surface.

items.title: Built ClaudeContext
items.body: Workflow + memory system around AI-assisted development. Context-driven sessions, Jira/Toggl/RAG integration, MCP server for structured code intelligence. Ongoing — getting iterated on continuously.

items.title: Started ContextOS
items.body: Backend-and-frontend stack experiment: .NET 10 API with Hot Chocolate GraphQL + EF Core + PostgreSQL with pgvector for semantic search, paired with a React 19 + TypeScript + Apollo Client frontend.

items.title: Started MeetingMonitor
items.body: Native macOS Swift app for monitoring meeting state — small, focused tool built around the daily-work pain point of context switching during calls.
